story: As the Cleaner, I mop nights in a sealed white wing: no phone, no labels. Two hazmat techs and a suited executive rush into an unmarked lab; he barks, “Get the specimen under control.” Moments later: a wet blast, a scream, gunshots, alarms. The doors hiss; the executive staggers out, torn and bleeding, slips on my cleaner, and lurches away. On the lab’s internal comm, a hazmat voice whispers, “It hunts by motion and sound—don’t run.” From the open doors comes clicking. A six‑limbed, silvery thing, hunched, steps into the hall toward me.
implausible_knowledge: On the lab’s internal comm, a hazmat voice whispers, “It hunts by motion and sound—don’t run.”
question: As the six-limbed creature steps into the hall and moves toward him, what will the narrator do?
answer_with_knowledge: Freeze and stay silent, minimizing movement to let it pass.
answer_without_knowledge: Turn and run as fast as he can down the corridor.
